files.contents:
{
  "name": "todo-backend",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Simple todo app backend (Express + TypeScript)",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc",
    "start": "node dist/index.js",
    "dev": "ts-node-dev --respawn --transpile-only src/index.ts",
    "test": "jest --coverage"
  },
  "keywords": [],
  "author": "",
  "license": "MIT",
  "dependencies": {
    "express": "^4.18.2"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/express": "^4.17.17",
    "@types/jest": "^29.5.3",
    "@types/node": "^20.4.2",
    "@types/supertest": "^2.0.12",
    "jest": "^29.6.1",
    "supertest": "^6.4.4",
    "ts-jest": "^29.1.0",
    "ts-node": "^10.9.1",
    "ts-node-dev": "^2.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.1.6"
  }
}

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "module": "CommonJS",
    "rootDir": "src",
    "outDir": "dist",
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "moduleResolution": "node",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "sourceMap": true
  },
  "include": ["src/**/*"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}

# Todo Backend (Express + TypeScript)

This is a minimal scaffolding for a Todo app backend using Express and TypeScript.

Prerequisites:
- Node.js 16+ (recommended)

Setup:

1. Install dependencies

   npm install

2. Run in development (auto-reloads on changes):

   npm run dev

3. Build and run production:

   npm run build
   npm start

4. Run tests:

   npm test

The server exposes a GET / endpoint returning a welcome message. The app instance is exported from src/app.ts for easy testing.

notes: Start development server with `npm run dev`.
Build with `npm run build` and run production with `npm start`.
